ticket: A 39-year-old G3P2 (advanced maternal age) presents: G2P1, GDM, EFW 4200g, prolonged second stage, head delivered but no restitution. Provide a comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: shoulder dystocia. Key management: McRoberts maneuver, suprapubic pressure, episiotomy.

ticket: A 20-year-old G1P0 (young primigravida) presents: 30 weeks, regular contractions q5min, cervix 3cm/80%, positive fFN. Provide a comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: preterm labor. Key management: tocolytics, betamethasone, magnesium for neuroprotection.

ticket: A 31-year-old G2P1 presents: 12 weeks, intractable vomiting despite antiemetics, requiring IV fluids, hypokalemia. Which of the following is the MOST likely diagnosis?

ticket: A 19-year-old G1P0 (young primigravida) presents: 12 weeks, intractable vomiting despite antiemetics, requiring IV fluids, hypokalemia. Provide a comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.

ticket: A 33-year-old G4P3 (grand multipara) presents: 28 weeks, pelvic pressure, cervical shortening to 15mm on TVUS, mild contractions. Provide a comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: preterm labor. Key management: tocolytics, betamethasone, magnesium for neuroprotection.

ticket: A 23-year-old G1P0 (primigravida) presents: 30 weeks, 3-hour GTT abnormal (2/4 values elevated), no prior diabetes history. Provide a comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.

correct_answer: B

ticket: A 40-year-old G3P2 (advanced maternal age) presents: 32 weeks, painless vaginal bleeding, ultrasound shows complete previa. Provide a comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.

ticket: A 22-year-old G1P0 (young primigravida) presents: 1 hour postpartum, steady bleeding from perineal laceration, well-contracted uterus. Provide a comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: postpartum hemorrhage. Key management: uterine massage, uterotonics, transfusion.
